text: For midportion Achilles tendinopathy, all active treatment classes appear superior to wait-and-see at 3-month follow-up.
There is no clinically relevant difference in effectiveness between different active treatments for midportion Achilles tendinopathy at either 3-month or 12-month follow-up.

Exercise therapy, exercise plus injection therapy, and exercise plus night splint therapy are all comparable in effectiveness to injection therapy alone for midportion Achilles tendinopathy at 12 months.
Calf-muscle exercise programmes are recommended as first-line treatment for midportion Achilles tendinopathy due to low cost, ease of prescription, and few harms.
Wait-and-see is not recommended for midportion Achilles tendinopathy as active treatment produces better outcomes.

The majority of Achilles tendinopathy cases in clinical trials involve midportion tendinopathy rather than insertional tendinopathy, with insufficient evidence to perform network meta-analysis for insertional Achilles tendinopathy.
